clusters.title: France confirms first Ebola case in doctor who had worked in DRC
clusters.neutral_headline: France confirms first Ebola case in doctor who had worked in DRC
bullet_summary: The patient was transferred to a specialist facility and was in a stable condition, the ministry said in a statement
France on Wednesday announced its first confirmed case of Ebola identified on its territory, a doctor who had returned from the Democratic Republic of Congo
The health ministry said the patient has a "very low" viral load and that contacts are "currently being identified"
France has reported its first case of Ebola during the latest outbreak after a doctor returning from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) tested positive

items.title: France confirms first Ebola case in doctor who had worked in DRC
items.standfirst: French health ministry says patient’s contacts are being traced and that risk to European public is very lowThe first case of Ebola has been confirmed in France, the country’s health ministry has said, in a doctor who had returned from a humanitarian mission to an area affected by the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.The patient was transferred to a specialist facility and was in a stable condition, the ministry said in a statement. “All precautionary measures, including the patient’s isolation, were taken upon his arrival in the country, with transfer to the hospital under secure conditions to prevent any risk of contamination.” Continue reading...
items.content: French health ministry says patient’s contacts are being traced and that risk to European public is very lowThe first case of Ebola has been confirmed in France, the country’s health ministry has said, in a doctor who had returned from a humanitarian mission to an area affected by the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.The patient was transferred to a specialist facility and was in a stable condition, the ministry said in a statement. “All precautionary measures, including the patient’s isolation, were taken upon his arrival in the country, with transfer to the hospital under secure conditions to prevent any risk of contamination.” Continue reading...

items.title: France confirms first Ebola case
items.standfirst: More than 260 people in the Democratic Republic of Congo are known to have died during the current outbreak.
items.content: More than 260 people in the Democratic Republic of Congo are known to have died during the current outbreak.

items.title: France confirms first Ebola case in doctor returning from DR Congo
items.standfirst: The patient is in isolation, following strict biosafety protocols to prevent risk of spread in France.
items.content: The patient is in isolation, following strict biosafety protocols to prevent risk of spread in France.

items.title: France confirms first Ebola case in doctor returning from DR Congo mission
items.standfirst: France on Wednesday announced its first confirmed case of Ebola identified on its territory, a doctor who had returned from the Democratic Republic of Congo. The health ministry said the patient has a "very low" viral load and that contacts are "currently being identified".
items.content: France on Wednesday announced its first confirmed case of Ebola identified on its territory, a doctor who had returned from the Democratic Republic of Congo. The health ministry said the patient has a "very low" viral load and that contacts are "currently being identified".

items.title: Inside the gold-mining town where the Ebola outbreak likely started
items.standfirst: NPR reports from Mongbwalu in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The fight to contain the virus faces obstacles from lack of supplies to residents who doubt that the virus is real.
items.content: NPR reports from Mongbwalu in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The fight to contain the virus faces obstacles from lack of supplies to residents who doubt that the virus is real.(Image credit: Arsène Mpiana for NPR)

items.title: France reports first Ebola case
items.standfirst: France has reported its first case of Ebola during the latest outbreak after a doctor returning from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) tested positive.
items.content: France has reported its first case of Ebola during the latest outbreak after a doctor returning from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) tested positive.

clusters.title: Senate passes Iran war powers resolution in symbolic rebuke of Trump
clusters.neutral_headline: US Senate approves Iran war powers resolution: What that means for Trump
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Al Jazeera, France24 and others

items.title: Senate passes Iran war powers resolution in symbolic rebuke of Trump
items.standfirst: President Trump is facing growing backlash over the Iran war. On Tuesday, the Senate voted to limit the president's war powers and some lawmakers, including Republicans, have questioned the U.S. deal with Iran. Nikole Killion reports.
items.content: President Trump is facing growing backlash over the Iran war. On Tuesday, the Senate voted to limit the president's war powers and some lawmakers, including Republicans, have questioned the U.S. deal with Iran. Nikole Killion reports.

items.title: US Senate approves Iran war powers resolution: What that means for Trump
items.standfirst: Four Republicans cross party lines to vote in favour of the measure.
items.content: Four Republicans cross party lines to vote in favour of the measure.

items.title: US Senate passes symbolic rebuke to Trump over Iran war
items.standfirst: The US Senate passed a largely symbolic resolution Tuesday calling for an end to President Donald Trump's war with Iran, delivering a fresh rebuke to the White House as it tries to negotiate a lasting settlement with Tehran.
items.content: The US Senate passed a largely symbolic resolution Tuesday calling for an end to President Donald Trump's war with Iran, delivering a fresh rebuke to the White House as it tries to negotiate a lasting settlement with Tehran.

items.title: Congress passes war powers measure for first time, breaking with Trump over Iran
items.standfirst: The resolution passed on Tuesday was largely symbolic, but it adds to pressure on the White House to end the conflict once and for all.
items.content: The resolution passed on Tuesday was largely symbolic, but it adds to pressure on the White House to end the conflict once and for all.

items.title: Senate approves war powers resolution in symbolic move rebuking Trump on Iran
items.standfirst: The Senate approved a war powers resolution that rebukes President Trump by calling for an end to the war in Iran. While the vote is largely symbolic, it is a rare bipartisan pushback regarding the war.
items.content: The Senate approved a war powers resolution that rebukes President Trump by calling for an end to the war in Iran. While the vote is largely symbolic, it is a rare bipartisan pushback regarding the war.

items.title: Senate rebukes Trump by approving House-passed measure calling for end to Iran war
items.standfirst: The largely symbolic measure passed as the U.S. and Iran are in the early stages of talks aimed at ending the war.
items.content: The largely symbolic measure passed as the U.S. and Iran are in the early stages of talks aimed at ending the war.

items.content: All three candidates backed by NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani were projected to win their Democratic primaries Tuesday night.

items.content: Brad Lander has defeated two-term incumbent Congressman Dan Goldman for the Democratic nomination in New York's 10th Congressional District.

items.content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani endorsed three left-of-center candidates in the congressional Democratic primaries, and all three are set to win, CBS News projects.

items.content: Democrat Cait Conley will challenge Republican Rep. Mike Lawler in the battle for New York's 17th Congressional District, which encompasses many of New York City's northern suburbs.

clusters.title: France records hottest day ever as Europe suffers brutal heat wave
clusters.neutral_headline: Power outages in France as Europe bakes in record heat
bullet_summary: Reported by NBC World, France24, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: France records hottest day ever as Europe suffers brutal heat wave
items.standfirst: France followed its hottest ever day with yet more brutal temperatures Wednesday as a deadly heatwave scorches much of western Europe.
items.content: France followed its hottest ever day with yet more brutal temperatures Wednesday as a deadly heatwave scorches much of western Europe.

items.title: Hospitals on red alert as France sees hottest day ever
items.standfirst: France recorded its hottest day ever Tuesday as an extreme heat wave gripped Europe. With temperatures set to be scorching again on Wednesday, France 24's Andrew Hilliar reports from outside a Paris hospital, where he says there has already been an influx of patients suffering from heat-related illnesses.
items.content: France recorded its hottest day ever Tuesday as an extreme heat wave gripped Europe. With temperatures set to be scorching again on Wednesday, France 24's Andrew Hilliar reports from outside a Paris hospital, where he says there has already been an influx of patients suffering from heat-related illnesses.

clusters.title: Trains halted across Germany by communication system problem
clusters.neutral_headline: Germany rail network briefly halted nationwide due to IT malfunction
bullet_summary: Reported by Reuters Top News, BBC World, Sky News World and others

items.title: Trains halted across Germany by communication system problem
items.standfirst: A nationwide failure in Deutsche Bahn’s GSM-R rail communications system briefly stopped all trains across Germany, stranding passengers and forcing delays at stations. Service later resumed step by step after the outage was resolved. [apnews.com]
items.content: A nationwide failure in Deutsche Bahn’s GSM-R rail communications system briefly stopped all trains across Germany, stranding passengers and forcing delays at stations. Service later resumed step by step after the outage was resolved. [apnews.com]

items.title: Germany rail network briefly halted nationwide due to IT malfunction
items.standfirst: Rail company Deutsche Bahn had to pause train services across the country for more than two-and-a-half hours.
items.content: Rail company Deutsche Bahn had to pause train services across the country for more than two-and-a-half hours.

items.title: Trains across Germany briefly halted after communication system issue
items.standfirst: Germany's railway system was brought to a halt on Tuesday evening, leaving passengers stranded across the country following an IT issue.
items.content: Germany's railway system was brought to a halt on Tuesday evening, leaving passengers stranded across the country following an IT issue.

items.title: German Rail Services Disrupted After Comms System Failure
items.standfirst: Germany’s national rail operator is struggling to restore regular service after a communications system outage apparently caused by a replacement component brought trains to a nationwide standstill late Tuesday.
items.content: Germany’s national rail operator is struggling to restore regular service after a communications system outage apparently caused by a replacement component brought trains to a nationwide standstill late Tuesday.

items.title: U.N. nuclear agency chief says Iran inspections will happen, at some point

items.content: After conflicting remarks from Iran and the U.S., the U.N. nuclear agency chief says Iranian sites will be inspected, but the timing is "not essential."

items.content: The head of the U.N.'s nuclear agency has signaled that Iranian nuclear enrichment sites would be visited by his inspectors, a day after the U.S. and Iran offered contradictory remarks about the issue.(Image credit: Kyodo News)

clusters.title: Cristiano Ronaldo makes history as first player to score in 6 World Cups
clusters.neutral_headline: Watch World Cup Day 13: Portugal’s Ronaldo breaks record while England draw
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, France24, Al Jazeera

items.title: Cristiano Ronaldo makes history as first player to score in 6 World Cups
items.standfirst: Portugal's Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice on Tuesday, making him the first player ever to score goals in six World Cups. The final round of the tournament's group stage begins on Wednesday. Cristian Benavides reports.
items.content: Portugal's Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice on Tuesday, making him the first player ever to score goals in six World Cups. The final round of the tournament's group stage begins on Wednesday. Cristian Benavides reports.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Ronaldo breaks record, Ghana thwart England
items.standfirst: Cristiano Ronaldo silenced his critics as he became the first man to score in six different World Cups on Tuesday as Portugal thrashed Uzbekistan 5-0. while England could only managea 0-0 draw against Ghana in Monday's group games.
items.content: Cristiano Ronaldo silenced his critics as he became the first man to score in six different World Cups on Tuesday as Portugal thrashed Uzbekistan 5-0. while England could only managea 0-0 draw against Ghana in Monday's group games.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Ronaldo answers critics as Portugal get back on track
items.standfirst: Cristiano Ronaldo responded to criticism in the best possible way on Tuesday against Uzbekistan (5–0). The 41-year-old striker scored twice to lead his country to their first victory in this World Cup.
items.content: Cristiano Ronaldo responded to criticism in the best possible way on Tuesday against Uzbekistan (5–0). The 41-year-old striker scored twice to lead his country to their first victory in this World Cup.

clusters.title: World Cup 2026: Messi, Mbappe, Haaland contest best ever Golden Boot race?

bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, France24, NBC World

items.title: World Cup 2026: Messi, Mbappe, Haaland contest best ever Golden Boot race?
items.standfirst: Lionel Messi leads the way with five goals with Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland second with four goals each.
items.content: Lionel Messi leads the way with five goals with Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland second with four goals each.

items.title: All guns blazing as Messi, Mbappé and Haaland lead World Cup Golden Boot race
items.standfirst: Just Fontaine has reigned as the World Cup's record scorer at a single tournament since 1958. But with Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappé and Erling Haaland each piling on the goals for their respective countries, and the likes of Harry Kane and Cristiano Ronaldo in pursuit, the decades-old record is looking vulnerable for the first time.
items.content: Just Fontaine has reigned as the World Cup's record scorer at a single tournament since 1958. But with Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappé and Erling Haaland each piling on the goals for their respective countries, and the likes of Harry Kane and Cristiano Ronaldo in pursuit, the decades-old record is looking vulnerable for the first time.

items.title: Erling Haaland powers Norway into the next round and keeps pace in Golden Boot race
items.standfirst: After tallying four goals in his first two World Cup games, the Manchester City striker is challenging Lionel Messi and Kylian Mbappé for the honor.
items.content: After tallying four goals in his first two World Cup games, the Manchester City striker is challenging Lionel Messi and Kylian Mbappé for the honor.

items.content: About 68,000 homes in Brittany have been affected, with electricity unlikely to be fully restored until Wednesday night.

items.title: France outage leaves 68,000 homes without power as record heatwave spreads north
items.standfirst: A power outage caused by a record-breaking heatwave left around 68,000 households without electricity in France's western Brittany on Wednesday as the national weather agency issued a red alert for extreme weather covering most of the country, including the northernmost region around Calais.
items.content: A power outage caused by a record-breaking heatwave left around 68,000 households without electricity in France's western Brittany on Wednesday as the national weather agency issued a red alert for extreme weather covering most of the country, including the northernmost region around Calais.
